title: Noise-robust 1-copy distillation protocol for all distillable Bell-diagonal qutrits
authors: Tobias C. Sutter, Christopher Popp, Beatrix C. Hiesmayr
abstract: Entanglement distillation is the process of converting noisy entangled states into maximally entangled pure states via local operations and classical communication. A long-standing, unresolved question is which entangled states are amenable to distillation, known as the distillability problem. We solve this for Bell-diagonal qutrits with Weyl structure, and present a noise-robust scheme for entanglement distillation. In particular, we find that violating the positive partial transposition (PPT) criterion is necessary and sufficient for the 1-distillability of these states. For this, we construct a Schmidt rank 2 eigenvector of the partially transposed density matrix associated with its unique, three-fold degenerate negative eigenvalue. This feature makes the derived entanglement distillation protocol resilient to white-noise effects on the quantum states. Our results thus make noisy entangled qutrit pairs more accessible for future quantum technologies.
summary: This paper solves the distillability problem for a specific class of Bell-diagonal qutrit states, proving that any state violating the PPT criterion can be distilled using a single copy. The authors develop a new distillation protocol that uses local filters to transform qutrit states into qubit states. This protocol is specifically designed to be resilient to white noise by utilizing the properties of the partially transposed density matrix's eigenvectors. These findings make noisy, high-dimensional entangled states more useful for practical quantum technologies.
main_problem: Determining whether all NPT Bell-diagonal qutrit states are 1-distillable and constructing a noise-robust 1-copy distillation protocol for them.
main_result: The authors prove that for Bell-diagonal qutrit states with Weyl structure, violating the PPT criterion is both necessary and sufficient for 1-distillability, and they provide a noise-resilient distillation scheme.
method: The study uses a constructive proof involving the partial transpose and the construction of a Schmidt rank 2 witness vector based on the eigenvectors of the partially transposed density matrix.
model_or_system: Bipartite qutrit-qutrit systems (d=3) with Weyl structure, specifically Bell-diagonal states subject to white-noise-affected models.
key_observables: The expectation value of the partial transpose and the smallest eigenvalue of the partially transposed matrix.
important_parameters: The noise parameter p, the Schmidt rank of the witness vector, and the success probability q of the filtering process.
main_assumptions: The results are specifically applicable to Bell-diagonal qutrit states with Weyl structure and do not extend to general dimensions d > 3.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the distillability problem, defines the Weyl-structure qutrit model, provides a constructive proof for the sufficiency of PPT violation, details the construction of the noise-robust witness and protocol, and discusses limitations regarding higher dimensions.
why_interesting: This work provides a fundamental solution to a specific case of the distillability problem and offers a practical method for enhancing the robustness of entanglement against white noise, which is critical for quantum communication and networking.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
